stops.name: Centennial Park
stops.notes: Small town park with picnic area

stops.name: Centerville City Park
stops.notes: Shady park near the largest courthouse square in the U.S. Easy, walkable leg-stretch.

stops.name: Judge Lewis Park
stops.notes: Park on the western edge of town with a paved walking loop and restrooms.

stops.name: Pioneer Park
stops.notes: Right on Highway 2 between Clarinda and Shenandoah. Picnic shelters, restrooms, fishing pond. A classic drive-break spot; also has RV camping with hookups and a dump station.

stops.name: Arbor Lodge State Historical Park
stops.notes: Birthplace of Arbor Day. Large shaded arboretum and picnic grounds. Mansion open Tue-Sun 11am-5pm (grounds open daily).

stops.name: Crete City Park
stops.notes: Right on NE-33. Good midpoint between Nebraska City and Hastings.

stops.name: North Park
stops.notes: Shade trees, picnic tables, and a nice lake. Holdrege also has another park just south of US-34 on Burlington St. and an inexpensive, level city campground for overnighting on East Ave just south of the tracks. Open 24 hours.

stops.name: Barnett Park
stops.notes: Attractive town park on several small lakes a few blocks south of US-34. Hours: 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM.

stops.name: Ward Bond Memorial Park
stops.notes: Green and shady with picnic tables and pavilions. May have city RV sites.

stops.name: Yuma City Park
stops.notes: Town park near the Colorado-Nebraska border, six blocks from US-34.

stops.name: Riverside Park
stops.notes: A genuine oasis on the plains. Riverside nature trails, disc golf, large shaded picnic areas. Hours: 5:00 AM - 11:00 PM.

stops.name: Viestenz-Smith Mountain Park
stops.notes: Right on US-34 in the canyon. Shaded riverside picnic tables and a CCC-built shelter. Last good pause before the winding climb to Estes Park. Check seasonal opening before relying on it. Hours: 6:00 AM - 9:00 PM.

stops.name: Howard T. Sawhill City Park
stops.notes: Town-square park with a band shell, oval amphitheater, and central fountain.

stops.name: Lake Atwood
stops.notes: Small city lake just north of US-36 with shaded picnic areas and fishing; a quick, walkable break in Atwood.

stops.name: Oberlin City Park
stops.notes: In-town city park right off US-36 — a shaded spot for a quick leg-stretch in the middle of Oberlin.

stops.name: Elmwood Park
stops.notes: Norton's shaded city park with picnic tables and restrooms; a good plains stretch-break.

stops.name: Phillipsburg City Park
stops.notes: Shaded in-town park with picnic tables — a straightforward break in the county seat.

stops.name: Wagner Park
stops.notes: County-seat park near the historic Old Dutch Mill; mature shade trees and picnic tables make a pleasant midpoint stop.

stops.name: Mankato City Park
stops.notes: Quiet county-seat city park for a quick leg-stretch.

stops.name: Belleville City Park
stops.notes: Shaded in-town city park, an easy break along US-36.

stops.name: Washington City Park
stops.notes: City Park with three covered picnic shelters.

stops.name: Marysville City Park
stops.notes: Home of Marysville's famous black squirrels. Big shaded park with restrooms an old train engine.

stops.name: Seneca City Park
stops.notes: Shaded city park with shelter houses and restrooms near the west side of town; South Park on the south side also has a picnic shelter and restrooms.

stops.name: Hiawatha City Lake Park
stops.notes: City lake park near the US-36/US-73 junction with picnic areas and fishing; an easy stretch-break.

stops.name: Krug Park
stops.notes: Large historic city park with a lagoon, gardens, and shaded picnic areas.

stops.name: Lyons Park
stops.notes: Small town park on the far outskirts of Denver.

stops.name: Memorial Park
stops.notes: Shaded roadside grove and rest stop at the southwest corner of the US-36/SH-71 junction, with restrooms and picnic facilities. A famous oasis of trees on the treeless plains (a noted migrant-bird stopover).

stops.name: Cope Memorial Park
stops.notes: Cope's small town memorial park — a quick leg-stretch midway across the eastern Colorado plains.

stops.name: Veterans Memorial Park
stops.notes: Roadside park and rest area in tiny Joes with shade and picnic space.

stops.name: Bittersweet Park
stops.notes: Greeley's shaded city park with a small lake, walking paths, and picnic areas.

stops.name: Akron City Park
stops.notes: Tiny town park three blocks from US-34.

stops.name: Rainbow Park
stops.notes: Tree-shaded town park with covered picnic shelters and an extensive paved multi-use trail along the North Fork of the Republican River, right off US-34.

stops.name: Trenton Park
stops.notes: Small town park for a quick leg-stretch; Swanson Reservoir is just west of town for a longer break but requires entry permit.

stops.name: Cambridge City Park
stops.notes: Shaded city park in Cambridge in the Republican River valley — a good stop midway between McCook and Holdrege.

stops.name: Arapahoe South Park
stops.notes: Arapahoe's South Park: shaded city park with picnic space and a small camping area, an easy break on the US-6/US-34 run.

stops.name: Chautauqua Park
stops.notes: Town park near the railroad station with picnic tables and shade trees.

stops.name: Heartwell Park
stops.notes: Large shaded city park with a lagoon and picnic areas.  Two small parking lots, one on Lakeside Drive, the other on Forest Blvd.

stops.name: Sutton City Park
stops.notes: Shaded city park in Sutton, right on US-6 — an easy first break east of Hastings.

stops.name: Dorchester City Park
stops.notes: Small-town park in Dorchester on US-6

stops.name: Taggart Park
stops.notes: Town park in Palmyra along NE-2 between Lincoln and Nebraska City.

stops.name: Williams Park
stops.notes: Syracuse's city park, a short hop off the NE-2 bypass (N-50 exit) — the main town stop between US-77 and Nebraska City.

stops.name: Sidney City Park
stops.notes: Sidney's city park, just off the IA-2/US-275 bypass

stops.name: Clarinda City Park
stops.notes: City park in Clarinda (Glenn Miller's hometown) on IA-2 between Shenandoah and Bedford.

stops.name: Bibbins Park
stops.notes: Town park in Bedford, the Taylor County seat, on IA-2.

stops.name: Noel Park
stops.notes: City park in Leon on IA-2 (US-69 junction) between Mount Ayr and Corydon.

stops.name: Corydon Lake Park
stops.notes: Beautiful, quiet, clean park on a lake at the western edge of Corydon right off of IA-2.

stops.name: Roddy Road Park
stops.notes: Frederick County park beside the 1856 Roddy Road covered bridge, just off US-15 north of Thurmont — picnic shelter, tables, grills, and Owens Creek access.

stops.name: Mason-Dixon Welcome Center
stops.notes: Maryland welcome center on southbound US-15 just south of the Pennsylvania line — restrooms, vending, pet area, picnic tables, RV parking, and a Catoctin Mountains overlook.

stops.name: Point of Rocks Community Commons Park
stops.notes: Small community park by the historic Point of Rocks train station on the Potomac — picnic shelter, restrooms, trails, amphitheater, and C&O Canal towpath access.

stops.name: South Mountain Eastbound Welcome Center
stops.notes: Maryland welcome center on I-70 eastbound atop South Mountain (mile 39) — restrooms, playground, travel info, and RV/truck/car parking.

stops.name: South Mountain Westbound Welcome Center
stops.notes: Maryland welcome center on I-70 westbound atop South Mountain (mile 39) — restrooms, playground, travel info, and RV/truck/car parking.

stops.name: Crystal Spring Rest Area (I-70)
stops.notes: Pennsylvania rest area on I-70 eastbound (signed East, but runs south toward Hancock/MD) — the first rest area south of Breezewood. Restrooms, vending, and picnic tables.
